current_state: Functional prototype with local persistent memory. Agents execute autonomously in a Builder/Fixer dynamic driven by robustness metrics. The system maintains an episodic memory across sessions and generates telemetry graphs.
dependencies: pytest (for running test suites and extracting pass rates)
requests (for HTTP communication with local Ollama API)
ollama (local inference engine running qwen2.5-coder:7b or similar)

architecture.description: A single-agent orchestrator that switches personas based on code quality metrics. It uses a read-eval-propose-test loop.
architecture.system_graph_mermaid:
graph TD
    A[main loop] --> B{Load Memory}
    B --> C[Evaluate Baseline]
    C --> D{Decide Role}
    D -- Robustness >= 0.7 --> E[Builder Persona]
    D -- Robustness < 0.7 --> F[Fixer Persona]
    E --> G[LLM Proposal]
    F --> G
    G --> H[Parse JSON & Clean Newlines]
    H --> I[Write to Disk]
    I --> J[Run Pytest]
    J --> K[Calculate Robustness]
    K --> L{Acceptance Rules}
    L -- Pass --> M[Save Snapshot & Append Episode]
    L -- Fail --> N[Revert Files & Log Semantic Error]
    M --> O[Save Memory JSON]
    N --> O
    O --> D

logic: 1. Boot: Ensure files exist and load persistent memory (swarm_memory.json).
2. Baseline: Calculate initial pass_rate and robustness_score.
3. Role Decision: If robustness < 0.7, agent becomes 'Fixer' (adds try/except, typing). Otherwise, 'Builder' (adds new math operations).
4. Prompting: LLM receives current state, test failures, done ops, and source code.
5. Generation: LLM proposes new operations.py and test_basic.py contents via JSON tools.
6. Execution: The script writes the files and runs pytest.
7. Evaluation: Strict acceptance rules. Builders must pass tests. Fixers must increase robustness by 0.05 without breaking tests.
8. Persistence: Successes are logged as episodes in swarm_memory.json. Failures are stored semantically to avoid repetition.

files.core/operations.py: Target code file modified by the agent.
files.core/calculator.py: Auto-generated file exposing operations as a string calculator.
files.tests/test_basic.py: Target test file modified by the agent.
files.logs/agent_memory.log: Human-readable trace of LLM reasoning and decisions.
files.logs/pytest_debug.log: Full stdout/stderr of pytest runs for debugging.
files.experiments/: Snapshots of every accepted code version.

functions_definition.name: load_memory(base_score)
functions_definition.purpose: Deserializes state from swarm_memory.json to cure agent amnesia across sessions. Synchronizes base score.

functions_definition.name: save_memory(state)
functions_definition.purpose: Serializes the current state dictionary to JSON on disk.

functions_definition.name: ensure_files()
functions_definition.purpose: Bootstraps the project structure, creating initial directories, empty logs, and base code files if missing.

functions_definition.name: regenerate_calculator()
functions_definition.purpose: Parses operations.py and dynamically creates a calculator.py wrapper mapping operation strings to functions.

functions_definition.name: robustness_score()
functions_definition.purpose: AST-based static analyzer. Calculates a 0.0-1.0 score based on presence of try/except, type hints, isinstance, and if-raise guards.

functions_definition.name: run_tests()
functions_definition.purpose: Executes pytest. Captures full logs for debugging, and returns the pass rate along with the semantic failure traceback.

functions_definition.name: save_snapshot(fn, rate, robust, itype)
functions_definition.purpose: Backs up the accepted operations.py and test_basic.py into an experiments/exp_NNN folder.

functions_definition.name: _decide_role()
functions_definition.purpose: Determines if the LLM should act as a Builder (new functions) or Fixer (robustness) based on the ROBUSTNESS_FIXER_THRESHOLD.

functions_definition.name: _failure_memory()
functions_definition.purpose: Extracts the latest test tracebacks for failed functions to inject into the LLM prompt, preventing blind loops.

functions_definition.name: ask(client)
functions_definition.purpose: Constructs the dynamic prompt and calls the Ollama (or Anthropic) API to get the tool-use JSON proposal.

functions_definition.name: main()
functions_definition.purpose: The core loop. Orchestrates the flow, applies patches, evaluates results, and handles accept/reject logic.

functions_definition.name: plot_results()
functions_definition.purpose: Uses matplotlib to read all experiment metadata and generate a telemetry chart of pass_rate, robustness, and score.
